calendar.matters:
Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Striking Costs (Taxing Costs) Cfc Rule 3.1700 (B); Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declaration Of Jennifer Sommer Of Motion And
Off Calendar Sep-30-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances via Zoom Conference
Jacquelyn A. ruiz, Esq., LAW OFFICES OF LEONARD S. BECKER, 510-886-1996, for Plaintiff Michelle Medici..
Jennifer Sommer, Esq., CARBONE, SMITH & KOYAMA, (510) 267-7200, jennifer.sommer@csaa.com for Defendants, Alyce Vaughn, Lionel Vaughn.

ARGUED. Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows::

DEFENDANT'S ALYCE VAUGHN and LIONEL VAUGHN'S Motion For Order Striking Costs (Taxing Costs) Cfc Rule 3.1700.

Defendant's motion to strike/tax costs is denied.

The court concludes that the adduced costs (item 16 [mediation fees ($980)]; [medical records ($90.24)] were reasonably necessary to the conduct of the litigation and therefore recoverable. (See CCP 1033.5(c)(4); Gibson v. Bobroff (1996) 49 Cal.App.4th 1202, 1209 ["Encouraging the parties to resolve lawsuits at the earliest time and before a costly and time-consuming trial, is a necessary part of litigation as conducted in this state."].)

Order is signed.

Judge: Vedica Puri, Clerk: W. Trupek; Not Reported (302/VP)
